step.title: Workflow Design
step.goal: Design the workflow steps. Define the user journey through the skill: what happens at each step, what output is produced, where are the checkpoints.
step.note: This step only applies to workflow-type skills. Exec and data skills skip to step 8.

substep.title: Define Step Sequence
substep.ask:
Let's design the workflow steps. For each step, define: 1. Step number and title 2. Goal (what this step accomplishes) 3. Key actions or questions 4. Output (what gets saved via template-output)
Guidelines: - 3-8 steps total (4-6 is sweet spot) - Each step should have a clear deliverable - Put critical decisions early - Validation/completion always last

substep.title: Identify Checkpoints
substep.action: For each step, determine: - Does it have a template-output? (checkpoint where user reviews) - Is it critical="true"? (cannot be skipped even in YOLO) - Is it optional="true"? (can be skipped) - Does it have conditions? if="condition"

substep.title: Map Dependencies
substep.action: Identify dependencies between steps: - What variables does each step need from previous steps? - Are there conditional paths? (if type == X, do Y) - Which steps must complete before others can start?

substep.title: Confirm Workflow Design
substep.template-output:
Present the workflow design:
Table of steps with: step number, title, goal, checkpoint (yes/no). Identify any conditional steps (those gated by if="" conditions). Identify any critical="true" steps (cannot be skipped in YOLO mode).
[a] Redesign [c] Continue [y] YOLO

rules.rule: Each step should have ONE clear goal

pattern.name: Create Document
pattern.description: Guided creation of a structured document.
pattern.steps: 1. Discovery and Context (gather requirements) 2. Structure Decision (outline/type selection) [critical] 3. Scaffold (create file from template) 4-N. Fill Sections (one per major section) N+1. Validate and Complete

pattern.name: Analysis Workflow
pattern.steps: 1. Scope Definition (what to analyze) 2. Data Gathering (collect inputs) 3. Analysis (apply methodology) 4. Synthesis (combine findings) 5. Report Generation

pattern.name: Review Workflow
pattern.steps: 1. Load Subject (what to review) 2. Apply Checklist (systematic checks) 3. Findings Summary 4. Recommendations

section.title: Checkpoint Placement

rules.rule: After any significant output that user should review
NOT after trivial actions (loading a file)

section.example: Template-output after: discovery summary, section content, final validation
Template-output after: loading config file (trivial, no review needed)

section.title: Skills Invoked by Autonomous Callers
section.description: When a skill is invoked by a subagent (autonomous, no user interaction), the step files should NOT use <ask> tags or interactive checkpoints. The engine cannot pause for user input when the caller is autonomous.

differences.rule: template-output tags structure return output sections (not save-and-ask)
No <ask> tags — autonomous callers cannot answer
No [a] [c] [y] checkpoints — caller doesn't pause for review
Steps may route by input parameter rather than always being linear

section.title: Return-Signal Multiplicity for Autonomous Skills
section.description: Skills invoked by subagents or other skills often need to emit different return shapes for different runtime conditions (success vs missing-input vs runtime-condition-X). Express each return signal as its own <check if=""> block in the final step, with an <output-format> nested inside the matching condition.

signal-classes.signal: ERROR — input contract violated; caller bug. Names the missing/malformed field.
Success signal — work completed; structured payload.
Domain signals — runtime conditions the caller must route on (e.g., NEEDS_DECOMPOSITION, PLAN_DRIFT in code-implement's worker dispatch).

rules.rule: Validate inputs in step 1; emit ERROR and stop if missing/malformed.
Each signal carries enough context for the caller to route without re-running the skill.
Distinguish ERROR (caller's fault) from domain signals (runtime condition).

section.example: Final step uses branched output: <check if="work completed"> <output-format>SUCCESS_SIGNAL ...</output-format> </check> <check if="domain condition X"> <output-format>DOMAIN_SIGNAL_X ...</output-format> </check>

section.title: Critical vs Optional Steps

rules.rule: critical="true": Cannot be skipped, even in YOLO mode
optional="true": Can be skipped (ask user unless YOLO)
Default: Required, but can be YOLO'd through

section.example: Step 2 "Skill Architecture" → critical (wrong choice = wrong skill) Step 6 "Advanced Options" → optional (nice to have, not essential)

example.name: agent-creator Workflow (5 steps)
example.config: | Step | Title | Goal | Checkpoint? | |------|-------|------|-------------| | 1 | Discovery and Context | Gather requirements | Yes | | 2 | Template Selection | Select includes by mode | Yes | | 3 | Scaffold and Configure | Create files + frontmatter | Yes | | 4 | Fill Content Templates | Walk through each template | Yes (critical) | | 5 | Validate and Complete | Run validation | Yes |
example.rationale: 5 steps: Discovery → Template Selection → Scaffold → Fill (critical) → Validate. Step 4 is critical because it produces the actual content. Each step produces reviewable output.

example.name: Minimal Workflow
example.config: | Step | Title | Goal | Checkpoint? | |------|-------|------|-------------| | 1 | Gather Input | Get user requirements | Yes | | 2 | Process | Apply transformation | No | | 3 | Present Output | Show and confirm result | Yes |
example.rationale: Minimal 3-step pattern: Input → Process → Output. Good for simple transformations that need user confirmation.
